name: Settin' 'Round Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the shortening and butter together. Allow them to cool slightly before adding to the dough to prevent killing the yeast.
2. If scalding milk, do so and let it cool (common practice is to scald milk before baking).
3. Dissolve yeast and sugar in 1/4 cup lukewarm water.
4. Mix cinnamon, oats, cornmeal, bran, cracked wheat, buckwheat, soy flour, and rye flour together.
5. Add remaining water, milk, butter, shortening, egg, honey to the mixture; stir well.
6. Stir in dissolved yeast mixture and salt.
7. Gradually add white flour until dough is stiff enough to knead (about 1/2 cup at a time).
8. Remove dough onto floured surface; knead adding more white flour if needed for workable consistency.
9. Knead until smooth and elastic, about ten minutes.
10. Place dough into lightly greased bowl; cover. Let rise in warm place (85°F is best) for 45 minutes, or until doubled in size.
11. Punch down dough; divide into two loaves. Shape each loaf and place into lightly greased loaf pans.
12. Allow to rise another 45 minutes while the bread continues rising.
13. Preheat oven to 350°F as bread finishes rising.
14. Bake bread for 35-40 minutes, or until it sounds hollow when tapped.
15. Remove from loaf pans; brush tops with butter for a soft, chewy crust.
